description: PFC command documentation index for quick lookup and LLM-assisted command discovery

ball.description: Ball object creation, modification, and management commands

wall.description: Wall object creation, modification, and management commands

clump.description: Clump object creation, modification, and template management commands

contact.description: Contact and contact model configuration commands

model.description: Model system control and configuration commands

measure.description: Measurement sphere creation and configuration commands

ball generate.reason: Python SDK doesn't support packing patterns for batch ball creation
ball generate.python_alternative: itasca.ball.create() - can create individual balls only
ball generate.python_workaround: Use itasca.ball.create() in a loop with manual positioning logic
use_cases: Generating large numbers of balls with packing patterns (cubic, hexagonal)
Creating initial assemblies with controlled porosity
Batch ball creation with size distributions

wall generate.reason: Python SDK doesn't support geometric wall generation (box, cylinder, etc.)
wall generate.python_alternative: Manual wall creation using Python SDK requires vertex-by-vertex construction
wall generate.python_workaround: Calculate vertices manually and use itasca.wall module

model solve.reason: Python SDK doesn't have automatic equilibrium detection
model solve.python_workaround: Use itasca.cycle() with manual ratio checking in a loop

contact model assign.reason: Bulk contact model assignment is more efficient via command
contact model assign.python_workaround: Use itasca.contact.list() with Contact.set_model() in loop
use_cases: Assigning contact models to all contacts or groups

ball attribute density.reason: Bulk attribute setting is more efficient via command
ball attribute density.python_alternative: Ball.set_density() - available but less efficient for bulk operations
ball attribute density.python_workaround: Use itasca.ball.list() with Ball.set_density() in loop
use_cases: Setting density for all balls or ball groups

fallback_hints.cubic packing: For cubic packing patterns, use 'ball generate cubic' command. Python SDK doesn't support this - manual positioning would be required.
fallback_hints.hexagonal packing: For hexagonal close packing, use 'ball generate hexagonal' command. Python SDK requires manual calculation of positions.
fallback_hints.wall box: To create box-shaped walls, use 'wall generate box' command. Python SDK requires manual vertex creation.
fallback_hints.wall cylinder: To create cylindrical walls, use 'wall generate cylinder' command. Python SDK requires manual vertex calculation.
fallback_hints.solve equilibrium: To solve to mechanical equilibrium, use 'model solve' command with ratio criteria. Python SDK itasca.cycle() requires manual convergence checking.
fallback_hints.contact model all: To assign contact models to all contacts, use 'contact model assign' command. More efficient than Python SDK iteration.
fallback_hints.automatic timestep: For automatic timestep calculation, use 'model auto dt' command. Python SDK itasca.set_timestep() sets manual values only.

notes: Commands are preferred over Python SDK for batch operations and specialized geometries
Python SDK is preferred for fine-grained control and programmatic logic
Use 'itasca.command()' to execute any PFC command from Python
Commands return no value; use 'result' subcommands to query results
Most commands operate on ranges (groups, spatial regions, all objects)
